title: Quiz: Angle Encoding
description: Test your understanding of angle encoding concepts

questions.question: In angle encoding, what does each classical feature value represent?

options.text: A rotation angle for a quantum gate

questions.explanation: In angle encoding, classical feature values are mapped to rotation angles (θ) of parameterized quantum gates like Rx, Ry, or Rz.

questions.question: How many qubits does parallel angle encoding require for n features?
questions.explanation: Parallel angle encoding uses one qubit per feature, so n features require n qubits. Each qubit is rotated by the corresponding feature value.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of the arcsin scaling function f(x) = 2·arcsin(x)?

options.text: It creates uniform distribution on the Bloch sphere

questions.explanation: The arcsin scaling function maps uniformly distributed inputs to a uniform distribution on the Bloch sphere, avoiding concentration near the poles.

questions.question: Sequential angle encoding can encode multiple features using only a single qubit.
questions.explanation: True. Sequential encoding applies multiple rotations to a single qubit, allowing multiple features to be encoded. However, this may reduce expressibility compared to parallel encoding.

questions.question: Why is angle encoding particularly well-suited for variational quantum algorithms?

options.text: It uses fewer qubits than other methods
It uses the same parameterized gates as variational layers
It has no periodic wrapping issues

questions.explanation: Angle encoding naturally integrates with variational circuits because both use parameterized rotation gates. This makes the combined circuit architecture uniform and easy to implement.
